title: fix: strip temperature/top_p/top_k for claude-opus-4-7
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Anthropic deprecated `temperature`, `top_p`, and `top_k` on Claude Opus 4.7 — non-default values return 400 from Bedrock with `\`temperature\` is deprecated for this model`. Strip all three in `parameterProcessor` for any model whose id matches `claude-opus-4-7`.

Impact: 63 errors in the last 4h on `claude-opus-4.7` per Tinybird.

- Added tests covering the strip behavior and a regression guard so Opus 4.6 still passes `temperature` through.

gist.headline: Cooling down Claude Opus
gist.blurb: Claude Opus 4.7 decided it doesn't like temperature settings anymore, so we're automatically stripping them out behind the scenes. Your requests will now succeed without throwing errors!
gist.summary: Automatically strips deprecated temperature, top_p, and top_k parameters from Claude Opus 4.7 requests to prevent 400 errors from Bedrock.
gist.impact: Users can now safely send standard sampling parameters to Claude Opus 4.7 without their requests failing.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a thermometer (temperature) and control dials to protect a glowing server, while Polly the bee watches amused. Represents stripping out the deprecated parameters to keep the system happy.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan nomnom creature happily eating a pixelated thermometer and control dials while the cute bee mascot watches amused. A retro server rack nearby glows lime green (#ecf874) with the word 'CLAUDE' on a small CRT screen. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
